sections.paragraph:
βραδέως adv. of βραδύς (Thu. et al.; Epict. 1, 18, 19; pap, e.g. PFay 97, 37; POxy 1088, 50; 2 Macc 14:17; Jos., Bell. 3, 274; Tat. 32, 2) slowly β. ἐγένετο it took a long time Hs 9, 6, 8.—DELG s.v. βραδύς.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 183
